title: Mellow Golden Irish Lamb Tagine
description: A savory and fluffy Irish dish featuring white cornmeal, cooked in a tagine and suitable for a Buddhist temple gathering, perfect for a date night.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, mix together the cornmeal, cumin, paprika, salt, and pepper.
Add the lamb pieces to the bowl and toss to coat evenly with the cornmeal mixture.
Heat the olive oil in the bottom of a clay or ceramic tagine or a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion to the tagine and cook until it is softened and lightly browned.
Add the minced garlic to the tagine and cook for 1 minute.
Add the lamb pieces to the tagine and cook until they are browned on all sides.
Add the chicken broth, dates, and prunes to the tagine, covering the lamb pieces completely.
Cover the tagine with a lid and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Bake the tagine for 2 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender and the sauce has thickened.
Remove the tagine from the oven and let it cool for 10 minutes before serving.
